rulings.Case Title: GODOFREDO PIQUE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Honeywell International¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ request for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY ADAMSON, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 1, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s special demurrers to the sixteenth (brake shoe grinding machine-negligence) and seventeenth (brake shoe grinding machine-product liability) causes of action are sustained without leave to amend. A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable because the power tools at issue in that case had no function until they were used with toxic-containing disks. (Id. at 584 (¿[U]se with the specified wheels, discs, and grinders was the inevitable use.¿). Also, in Tellez-Cordova, the allegations were that the wheels and discs were harmless without the power supplied by respondents¿ tools, while here the allegations are that Hennessy¿s machines were used on asbestos-containing brakes. It is the asbestos content of a product that makes it inherently dangerous. (Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 564.) Defendant¿s machines are more analogous to the stove in Garman. As in that case, it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. The only alleged defect in Hennessy¿s machines is that they caused another defective product to injure the decedent. The defendant has no duty under these facts. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
